tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst. Zdecyduj, ktore zdania (1.1-1.8) sa zgodne z trescia tekstu (T – True), ktore sa niezgodne (F – False), a ktore zawieraja informacje niepodane w tekscie (NI – No Information).
tasks.text:
THE OLYMPIC GAMES

The Olympic Games are the world's most important international sporting event. They bring together athletes from over 200 countries who compete in a wide variety of sports. The modern Olympic Games were started in 1896 in Athens, Greece, by a Frenchman named Pierre de Coubertin. He was inspired by the ancient Greek Olympic Games, which were held in Olympia from 776 BC and continued for over 1,000 years before being banned by the Roman Emperor Theodosius I in 393 AD.

The modern Olympics take place every four years, and the host city is chosen by the International Olympic Committee (IOC), which is based in Lausanne, Switzerland. There are two types of Olympic Games: the Summer Olympics and the Winter Olympics. Until 1992, both events were held in the same year. After that, they were separated, so now they alternate every two years.

One of the most recognisable symbols of the Olympics is the five coloured rings — blue, yellow, black, green, and red — on a white background. These rings represent the five continents that participate in the Games: Africa, the Americas, Asia, Europe, and Oceania. The Olympic flag with this design was first used at the 1920 Games in Antwerp, Belgium.

The Olympic torch relay is another important tradition. Before each Games, a flame is lit in Olympia, Greece, using the rays of the sun. The torch is then carried by thousands of runners across many countries until it reaches the host city, where it lights the Olympic cauldron during the opening ceremony. The flame burns throughout the Games and is put out at the closing ceremony.

Athletes who finish first, second, or third in their events receive gold, silver, and bronze medals. The gold medal is not actually made of solid gold — it is mostly silver with a thin layer of gold on top. Winning an Olympic medal is considered one of the greatest achievements in sport.

items.statement: Pierre de Coubertin came from Greece.
The ancient Olympic Games lasted for more than a thousand years.
The Summer and Winter Olympics have always been held in different years.
The headquarters of the IOC is located in Switzerland.
The Olympic flag was designed by Pierre de Coubertin himself.
The five Olympic rings stand for the five continents.
The Olympic torch is lit using sunlight in Greece.
The Olympic gold medal is made entirely of gold.
